title: Add e2e test for Keystone CR in PSS-restricted namespace
description:
# GitHub Issue #317: test(e2e): Reconcile Keystone CR in PSS-restricted namespace (#277 Phase 1)

## Context

Parent: #277, Phase 1 → *Security / compliance baseline*.

The operator already builds restricted PodSecurityContexts everywhere it spawns a Pod —
`operators/keystone/internal/controller/security_context.go:21` (`restrictedSecurityContext`)
is referenced from:

- `reconcile_deployment.go:174` (Keystone API Deployment)
- `reconcile_bootstrap.go:157` (bootstrap Job)
- `reconcile_database.go:806` (db-sync, db-expand, db-migrate, schema-check Jobs)
- `reconcile_fernet.go:315/328` (rotation init/main containers)
- `reconcile_credential.go:318/331` (rotation init/main containers)
- `reconcile_trustflush.go:124` (trust flush CronJob)
- `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:174` (policy validation Job)

The chart's operator pod itself sets pod- and container-level restricted contexts in
`helm/keystone-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` (lines 22-27 and 67-78). Unit tests
(`security_context_test.go`, `reconcile_bootstrap_test.go:319`,
`reconcile_fernet_test.go:558`, `reconcile_credential_test.go:565`) verify each call site.

What's missing: an end-to-end gate that proves the **whole** workload — operator + Keystone API
+ every Job and CronJob — admits cleanly into a namespace labelled
`pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted`. A regression where any future reconciler
forgets `restrictedSecurityContext()` would be caught by the API server's PSS admission, but
only if exercised at this enforcement level, which today's tests do not do.

## Scope — new test

A single Chainsaw test directory: `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/`.

Scenario:

1. Create a fresh test namespace labelled `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted, pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest`.
2. Apply a Keystone CR using the basic-deployment fixture, with `database.clusterRef` and `cache.clusterRef` pointing at the existing `openstack-db` / `openstack-memcached` (which already live in the `openstack` namespace and are unaffected by the new namespace's PSS label).
3. Trigger one Fernet rotation manually (`kubectl create job --from=cronjob/<name>-fernet-rotation manual-rotation -n <test-ns>`) so the rotation Pod is exercised under the restricted policy, not just the API Deployment.

Assertion:

- `(conditions[?type=='Ready']).status == \"True\"`, reason `AllReady`, within 5 min
- `kubectl get events -n <test-ns> --field-selector reason=FailedCreate` returns zero entries with a message containing `violates PodSecurity \"restricted:latest\"`
- The manual rotation Job reaches `succeeded > 0`

Out of scope: PSS at the **audit** or **warn** levels (Kubernetes default-on for any unlabelled
namespace already issues warn-level audit lines). Audit-level coverage is automatic; the
restricted enforce gate is the regression net.

## Out of scope (prerequisites, separate tickets)

None — every Pod spec the reconciler creates already sets `restrictedSecurityContext()`. The
test exercises the existing surface; if a future Pod is added without the helper, this test
catches it on the first failed admission.

## Test design

```
tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/
├── 00-namespace.yaml         (Namespace with PSS labels)
├── 01-keystone-cr.yaml       (CR in the new namespace, basic-deployment fixture)
└── chainsaw-test.yaml        (3 steps)
```

Steps:

1. `apply: 00-namespace.yaml` — Chainsaw's per-test namespace plumbing must be **disabled** for this test (`spec.namespace: \"\"` plus an explicit namespace in the manifest), because Chainsaw creates the implicit per-test namespace without PSS labels. Mirror the pattern from `tests/e2e/keystone/namespace-scoped-rbac/` (which also pins its namespace).
2. `apply: 01-keystone-cr.yaml` — assert `Ready=True`.
3. `script` step: trigger the manual rotation Job, wait for `kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/manual-rotation -n <test-ns> --timeout=2m`, then `kubectl get events -n <test-ns>` and `grep -v \"violates PodSecurity\"` and assert no matching events.

Catch block: `kubectl get pods -n <test-ns> -o yaml | grep -A3 securityContext`,
`kubectl get events -n <test-ns> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'`. PSS admission rejections produce
a `FailedCreate` event on the parent (Deployment, Job, CronJob) with the violation listed
verbatim, so the catch block surfaces the cause.

The test fits the `tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml` budget (5 min assert).

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted` green locally
- [ ] `make e2e` green; two consecutive `parallel: 4` CI runs without new flakes
- [ ] Sanity check: temporarily strip `restrictedSecurityContext()` from one reconciler in review, observe this test failing with the expected `violates PodSecurity` event
- [ ] No new infra in `deploy/kind/`; no chart changes; no operator code changes
- [ ] Parent #277: the one covered checkbox ticked
- [ ] Commit prefix `test(CC-xxxx)` once a feature code is assigned

## Risks / open questions

- **Cross-namespace MariaDB/Memcached references**: the CR points its `database.clusterRef` at `openstack/openstack-db`. The MariaDB operator places the resulting DB CRs in the cluster's namespace (the operator's own choice), so the PSS label on the test namespace does not affect them. If a future operator change moves DB CR creation into the consumer's namespace, this test would also need that namespace to admit MariaDB/Memcached operator pods — handle when it happens.
- **Pre-existing operator pod**: the `keystone-operator` Deployment is in `openstack`, not in the test namespace, so PSS does not gate it. This test is about *workload* Pods. A separate follow-up could add a PSS test for the operator chart by installing a release into a restricted namespace; out of scope here.

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/317

stories.title: Operator developer gets a regression net for missing restrictedSecurityContext()
stories.want: an e2e gate that fails when any Pod created by a Keystone reconciler omits restrictedSecurityContext()
stories.so_that: a future reconciler that forgets the helper is caught by CI before merge instead of in production
criteria: Test exists at tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/chainsaw-test.yaml and is collected by `chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/...`
Test exercises at least the API Deployment, bootstrap Job, db-sync Job, policy-validation Job, and one Fernet rotation Pod under the restricted profile
Removing `restrictedSecurityContext()` from any one reconciler causes this test to fail with an event matching `violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"`
Test run does not modify deploy/kind/, the keystone-operator chart, or any operator Go file

stories.title: Security/compliance reviewer can prove restricted-profile compatibility end-to-end
stories.want: a reproducible test that admits the full Keystone workload into a `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` namespace
stories.so_that: the Phase 1 security/compliance baseline (#277) has an executable check, not just unit-level evidence
criteria: Test namespace carries both `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` and `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest` labels
Chainsaw's per-test namespace plumbing is disabled (`spec.namespace: ""`) so the labelled namespace is the one Pods land in
Test asserts Ready=True with reason `AllReady` within the 5-minute budget configured in tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml
Test asserts zero events whose `reason=FailedCreate` carries `violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"` in the test namespace

stories.title: Developer triaging a CI failure can see which Pod tripped admission
stories.want: the catch block to surface pod securityContext, parent-controller events, and Job/pod logs
stories.so_that: I can identify the offending workload without re-running the test locally
criteria: On failure, catch block dumps `kubectl get pods -n <test-ns> -o yaml` (or equivalent securityContext extract)
On failure, catch block dumps `kubectl get events -n <test-ns> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'` so the FailedCreate row is visible
On failure, catch block dumps logs for any rotation/bootstrap/db-sync pods that did start
Catch block runs each diagnostic with `|| true` so a missing resource does not short-circuit the dump

stories.title: CI maintainer keeps the suite stable under parallel:4
stories.want: the new test to fit inside the suite-wide 5-minute assert budget and not race other tests
stories.so_that: two consecutive `parallel: 4` runs stay green and the suite does not gain a flaky entry
criteria: `spec.timeouts.assert` does not exceed 5m (matches sibling tests and tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml)
Brownfield Database/User/Grant CRs use a unique resource-name and database-name prefix (e.g. `keystone-pss-restricted-*`) to avoid collisions with sibling tests in the openstack namespace
Keystone CR uses a unique name (e.g. `keystone-pss-restricted`) so its derived resources (Secrets, Roles, CronJobs) do not collide with other Keystone CRs
PushSecret backup paths (per-CR `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{name}/{fernet,credential}-keys`) do not collide with any other CR in the suite

stories.title: Reader of the e2e test catalogue finds the new test documented
stories.role: reader of the e2e test catalogue
stories.want: a Reference entry in docs/reference/testing/keystone-e2e-tests.md describing the test, its purpose, and the assertion shape
stories.so_that: I can understand what the suite covers without reading the YAML
criteria: Test appears in the inventory table with a one-line purpose summary
A `### pod-security-restricted` section documents file paths, steps, and assertion shape (mirroring sibling sections)
The Diataxis reference doc explains the choice to use brownfield mode (database.host + cache.servers) and pinned namespace (`spec.namespace: ""`)
Cross-link to the parent issue/feature (CC-0104, #317, #277 Phase 1) is present

requirements.description: The test SHALL create a dedicated namespace labelled `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` AND `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest` and pin Chainsaw's per-test namespace via `spec.namespace: ""`
requirements.rationale: Chainsaw injects an implicit per-test namespace without PSS labels; without disabling that and applying our own labelled namespace, Pods would land in the unlabelled namespace and never exercise restricted-profile admission. The pattern mirrors tests/e2e/keystone/namespace-scoped-rbac/.

scenarios.name: Namespace carries both labels
scenarios.when: the test fixture 00-namespace.yaml is applied
scenarios.then: the Namespace object exists with both labels set
and_then: kubectl get ns <name> -o jsonpath='{.metadata.labels}' contains both keys

scenarios.name: Chainsaw namespace plumbing is disabled
scenarios.when: the test runs
scenarios.then: spec.namespace in chainsaw-test.yaml is the empty string and every fixture pins `metadata.namespace` explicitly
and_then: no Pod created during the test lands in a chainsaw-generated `chainsaw-*` namespace

scenarios.name: Labels survive idempotent re-apply
scenarios.when: the test re-applies 00-namespace.yaml on a cluster where the namespace already exists
scenarios.then: the apply succeeds and the labels remain set

requirements.description: The Keystone CR fixture SHALL use brownfield mode (`database.host`/`database.port` and `cache.servers`) — not `clusterRef` — so it can target the openstack-namespace MariaDB and Memcached without requiring those CRs in the test namespace
requirements.rationale: `Database.ClusterRef`/`Cache.ClusterRef` are `LocalObjectReference` and are looked up by `mariaDBResourceKey` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:70) in the same namespace as the Keystone CR. Cross-namespace lookup is not supported. Brownfield mode (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:250 ff.) is the only way to reach openstack/openstack-db without copying MariaDB/Memcached CRs into the test namespace. This contradicts the literal text of issue #317 but is the only path that matches the DoD constraint 'no new infra in deploy/kind/'.

scenarios.name: Brownfield database wiring
scenarios.when: the test applies 01-keystone-cr.yaml
scenarios.then: the CR has `spec.database.host=openstack-db.openstack.svc.cluster.local`, `spec.database.port=3306`, no `spec.database.clusterRef`
and_then: no Database/User/Grant CR named after the test CR is created in the test namespace by the reconciler (mirrors brownfield-database test assertion)

scenarios.name: Brownfield cache wiring
scenarios.when: the test applies 01-keystone-cr.yaml
scenarios.then: the CR has `spec.cache.servers=[openstack-memcached.openstack.svc:11211]`, no `spec.cache.clusterRef`

scenarios.name: MariaDB Database/User/Grant pre-created in openstack namespace
scenarios.when: 00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml is applied
scenarios.then: Database, User, and Grant CRs exist in the `openstack` namespace, reach `Ready=True`, and grant the test CR's user `ALL PRIVILEGES` on its database
and_then: resource and DB names are prefixed with `keystone-pss-restricted-` to avoid collisions with sibling tests

requirements.description: The test SHALL place the `keystone-admin` and `keystone-db` Secrets directly in the test namespace as plain Kubernetes Secrets (not ExternalSecrets), with values referenced from the existing openstack-namespace ExternalSecrets where possible
requirements.rationale: ExternalSecrets are namespaced; the operator's Bootstrap Job reads `keystone-admin` and the db-sync/User CR read `keystone-db` from the CR's namespace (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go and reconcile_database.go). Replicating ExternalSecrets adds OpenBao policy work outside our scope; pre-baking plain Secrets is the cheapest self-contained path and keeps the test independent of the OpenBao bootstrap state.

scenarios.name: Secrets exist before CR is applied
scenarios.when: the test applies 00-namespace.yaml (which contains the Namespace and the two Secrets)
scenarios.then: Secrets `keystone-admin` (key: password) and `keystone-db` (key: password) exist in the test namespace
and_then: secret values are deterministic test passwords (e.g. 32-char hex), documented inline as TEST-ONLY

scenarios.name: MariaDB User CR password matches test Secret
scenarios.when: the brownfield User CR is applied in the openstack namespace
scenarios.then: its `passwordSecretKeyRef` resolves to a Secret whose value matches the test `keystone-db` Secret in the test namespace
and_then: either the same Secret is also created in the openstack namespace, or the User CR references a dedicated `keystone-pss-restricted-db` Secret with the same password

scenarios.name: Bootstrap Job pulls admin password from test namespace
scenarios.when: the bootstrap Job runs
scenarios.then: it mounts `keystone-admin` from the test namespace and authenticates against Keystone

requirements.description: The test SHALL assert that the Keystone CR reaches `Ready=True` with reason `AllReady` within the 5-minute assert budget
requirements.rationale: This is the primary end-to-end signal that every reconciler-created Pod (API Deployment, bootstrap Job, db-sync, policy-validation) was admitted under restricted PSS. If any Pod is rejected, its parent controller surfaces FailedCreate and the relevant sub-condition stays False, blocking AllReady.

scenarios.name: Happy path: Ready=True/AllReady
scenarios.when: the test applies 01-keystone-cr.yaml after Steps 1-2 and waits within `spec.timeouts.assert: 5m`
scenarios.then: `(conditions[?type=='Ready']).status == 'True'` and `.reason == 'AllReady'`

scenarios.name: PSS rejection blocks AllReady
scenarios.when: any Pod parent (Deployment/Job/CronJob) is denied by PSS admission
scenarios.then: the corresponding sub-condition (DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady, DatabaseReady, PolicyValidReady) stays False and the assertion times out at 5m
and_then: the catch block runs and surfaces the FailedCreate event

scenarios.name: Unrelated transient failure does not promote false-positive PSS signal
scenarios.when: the assertion fails for a non-PSS reason (e.g. db-sync exit 1)
scenarios.then: the catch block dumps Job logs alongside events so the reviewer can distinguish PSS vs functional failure

requirements.description: The test SHALL trigger one manual Fernet rotation Job via `kubectl create job --from=cronjob/<name>-fernet-rotate` and SHALL assert the Job reaches `succeeded > 0` within 2 minutes
requirements.rationale: Reaching Ready=True does not exercise the rotation Pod (CronJob hasn't fired yet). The DoD requires that the rotation reconciler's Pod spec is also admitted. Mirrors the manual-rotation pattern from tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml.

scenarios.name: Manual rotation Job admits and completes
scenarios.when: `kubectl create job manual-rotation --from=cronjob/<name>-fernet-rotate -n <test-ns>` is run after Ready=True
scenarios.then: the Job's pod is admitted and reaches `status.succeeded > 0` within 2m

scenarios.name: PSS rejection on rotation Pod surfaces a FailedCreate event
scenarios.when: the rotation Pod's spec violates restricted PSS
scenarios.then: the manual-rotation Job's parent emits a FailedCreate event referencing the violation
and_then: the wait step times out and REQ-006's event scan picks up the violation message

scenarios.name: Job creation is idempotent under retry
scenarios.when: the create-job step is retried within the same chainsaw run
scenarios.then: the script names the Job deterministically (`<name>-pss-manual-rotate`) and skips creation if it already exists or deletes-then-recreates

requirements.description: The test SHALL assert that no event in the test namespace has `reason=FailedCreate` AND a message containing `violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"`
requirements.rationale: The PSS admission rejection emits a FailedCreate event on the Pod's parent (Deployment/Job/CronJob) with the violation listed verbatim. A zero-match scan is the explicit signal that no reconciler-created Pod tripped admission.

scenarios.name: Zero PSS-violation events on green
scenarios.when: the test runs to completion successfully
scenarios.then: `kubectl get events -n <test-ns> --field-selector reason=FailedCreate -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.message}{"\n"}{end}' | grep -F 'violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"'` returns no lines
and_then: the script exits 0 only when the grep finds zero matches; non-zero exit on any match

scenarios.name: Catch surfaces the violation when present
scenarios.when: any FailedCreate-with-PSS event exists
scenarios.then: the script exits non-zero, the catch block runs and prints the matching events with their parent object

scenarios.name: Scan scoped to the test namespace only
scenarios.when: the openstack namespace also has FailedCreate events for unrelated reasons
scenarios.then: the assertion does not pick those up because the field selector includes `-n <test-ns>`

requirements.description: The catch block SHALL dump pod securityContext, namespace events, and logs for any rotation/bootstrap/db-sync Job pods that started, each guarded with `|| true` so a missing resource does not abort the dump
requirements.rationale: Failure triage with parallel:4 means logs are interleaved across tests; the per-test catch block has to be self-contained so a reviewer can identify the offending workload from the chainsaw output alone.

scenarios.name: Catch block runs on assertion failure
scenarios.when: the Step 4 (Ready=True) or Step 6 (event scan) assertion fails
scenarios.then: the catch block prints pod yaml/securityContext, sorted events, and Job pod logs

scenarios.name: Missing resources do not short-circuit
scenarios.when: the catch block runs before the manual rotation Job exists
scenarios.then: the Job-specific dumps emit `|| true` and the rest of the catch block still runs

scenarios.name: Catch block scoped to test namespace
scenarios.when: the catch runs
scenarios.then: every kubectl invocation uses `-n <test-ns>` (no global queries that would dump the openstack namespace)

requirements.description: The test SHOULD use unique resource-name prefixes (`keystone-pss-restricted-*`, namespace `keystone-pss-restricted-test`) to avoid colliding with sibling tests under `parallel: 4`
requirements.rationale: tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml runs the suite with `parallel: 4` and `failFast: true`. Cluster-scoped or cross-namespace name collisions (Database/User/Grant in openstack namespace, OpenBao backup paths) would manifest as flakes.

scenarios.name: Cluster-scoped name uniqueness
scenarios.when: any cluster-scoped resource is created (none expected, but verify via grep)
scenarios.then: the test does not create cluster-scoped resources

scenarios.name: Brownfield CR names in openstack namespace are unique
scenarios.when: the brownfield Database/User/Grant CRs are applied in the openstack namespace
scenarios.then: their names start with `keystone-pss-restricted-` and the database `keystone_pss_restricted` does not collide with `keystone_basic`, `keystone_brownfield`, etc.

scenarios.name: PushSecret OpenBao paths are unique
scenarios.when: the operator creates the per-CR PushSecret
scenarios.then: the path `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-pss-restricted/{fernet,credential}-keys` is unique to this CR

requirements.description: The test SHALL be cataloged in docs/reference/testing/keystone-e2e-tests.md (Reference, Diataxis) with an inventory row and a per-test `### pod-security-restricted` section mirroring sibling sections
requirements.rationale: All other tests under tests/e2e/keystone/ are cataloged in this file; missing entries break the documented contract that 'the inventory is the source of truth for which scenarios the suite proves'.

scenarios.name: Inventory row exists
scenarios.when: docs/reference/testing/keystone-e2e-tests.md is rendered
scenarios.then: the inventory table contains a row `pod-security-restricted | keystone-pss-restricted | PSS restricted profile, brownfield wiring, manual rotation, zero PSS-violation events`

scenarios.name: Per-test section documents intent and shape
scenarios.when: a reader scrolls to `### pod-security-restricted`
scenarios.then: the section names the file paths, lists the steps in order, and explains the brownfield-mode + pinned-namespace decisions
and_then: cross-links to CC-0104, GitHub issue #317, and parent #277 Phase 1

scenarios.name: Inventory list ASCII art is updated
scenarios.when: the file is regenerated
scenarios.then: the ASCII inventory grid (lines 30-60 of the file) includes `pod-security-restricted` in the appropriate column

requirements.description: Verification: removing `restrictedSecurityContext()` from one reconciler MAY be performed locally (not in the committed test) to confirm the test fails with the expected `violates PodSecurity` event
requirements.rationale: The DoD calls out a manual sanity check. This is a verification step, not a committed test artifact; capturing it as a MAY requirement prevents it from leaking into the merged code.

scenarios.name: Negative-test sanity check passes
scenarios.when: a reviewer temporarily removes `restrictedSecurityContext()` from one reconciler (locally) and runs the test
scenarios.then: the test fails inside 5 min with a `FailedCreate` event whose message contains `violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"`
and_then: the offending reconciler is identifiable from the Pod parent object (Deployment / Job / CronJob name)

scenarios.name: Sanity check artifact does not leak
scenarios.when: the PR is opened
scenarios.then: no operator Go file is modified

tasks.title: Create tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/00-namespace.yaml: Namespace `keystone-pss-restricted-test` with `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` and `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest`, plus plain Secrets `keystone-admin` (key: password) and `keystone-db` (key: password) with TEST-ONLY values (32-char hex). Include SPDX header (REQ-001, REQ-003).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml in the openstack namespace: MariaDB Database (name: keystone-pss-restricted-db, db: keystone_pss_restricted), User (name: keystone-pss-restricted-user, passwordSecretKeyRef: keystone-pss-restricted-db-pw), Grant (name: keystone-pss-restricted-grant, ALL PRIVILEGES). Mirror tests/e2e/keystone/brownfield-database/00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml. Also include the `keystone-pss-restricted-db-pw` Secret with the same password as the test namespace's `keystone-db`. SPDX header. (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-008).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/01-keystone-cr.yaml: Keystone CR `keystone-pss-restricted` in `keystone-pss-restricted-test`, brownfield database (host=openstack-db.openstack.svc.cluster.local, port=3306, database=keystone_pss_restricted, secretRef=keystone-db), brownfield cache (servers=[openstack-memcached.openstack.svc:11211], backend=dogpile.cache.pymemcache), fernet rotationSchedule `0 0 * * 0`, bootstrap (admin/keystone-admin/RegionOne), replicas=1. SPDX header. (REQ-002).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/chainsaw-test.yaml skeleton: apiVersion v1alpha1, name `keystone-pod-security-restricted`, `spec.namespace: ""`, `spec.timeouts.assert: 5m`, SPDX + decision-style header comment summarising brownfield-mode + pinned-namespace + parent CC-0104/#317/#277 (REQ-001, REQ-008).
Add Step 1 to chainsaw-test.yaml: `apply: 00-namespace.yaml` then assert Namespace label `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted` is present (REQ-001).
Add Step 2 to chainsaw-test.yaml: `apply: 00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml` then assert Database/User/Grant CRs reach Ready=True in openstack namespace (mirror brownfield-database test) (REQ-002).

Add Step 3 to chainsaw-test.yaml: `apply: 01-keystone-cr.yaml`, assert `(conditions[?type=='Ready']).status='True', .reason='AllReady'` on the Keystone CR within the 5m budget. Add an `error` assertion that no Database/User/Grant CR named `keystone-pss-restricted` exists in the test namespace (brownfield-mode invariant) (REQ-002, REQ-004).

Add Step 4 to chainsaw-test.yaml: bash script that runs `kubectl create job keystone-pss-restricted-manual-rotate --from=cronjob/keystone-pss-restricted-fernet-rotate -n keystone-pss-restricted-test`, then `kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/... --timeout=2m`, then asserts `status.succeeded > 0`. Use `set -euo pipefail` and `shell: bash`. (REQ-005).

Add Step 5 to chainsaw-test.yaml: bash script that runs `kubectl get events -n keystone-pss-restricted-test --field-selector reason=FailedCreate -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.message}{"\n"}{end}'` and exits non-zero if any line contains the literal `violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"` (use `grep -F` and assert match count == 0). (REQ-006).

Add catch block(s) to Steps 3-5: dump pod securityContext (jsonpath extract), sorted events (last 30), bootstrap/db-sync/manual-rotate Job pod logs (current + previous), Job describe — every command guarded with `|| true`. Mirror catch shape from tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml. (REQ-007).

Update docs/reference/testing/keystone-e2e-tests.md: (a) add `pod-security-restricted` to the ASCII inventory grid (lines ~30-60), (b) add inventory-table row with CR name `keystone-pss-restricted` and purpose summary, (c) add `### pod-security-restricted` section with file paths, step-by-step table, brownfield-mode + pinned-namespace rationale, cross-link to CC-0104 / #317 / #277 Phase 1. (REQ-009).

Local verification: run `chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted` against a kind cluster brought up via `make e2e-up` (or equivalent). Confirm Ready=True, manual rotation Job succeeded, zero PSS-violation events. Capture timing (must finish well inside 5m).

Suite verification: run the full `make e2e` (parallel:4) twice consecutively; confirm zero new flakes and that the new test name appears in `_output/reports/chainsaw-report-*.xml`. Verify no name collisions with sibling tests in the openstack namespace.

Negative-test sanity check (manual, not committed): on a local working copy, temporarily remove `restrictedSecurityContext()` from one reconciler call site (e.g. reconcile_fernet.go:315), rebuild the operator image, re-run the test, observe FailedCreate event with `violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"`. Revert before opening the PR. Capture observation in the PR description. (REQ-010).

test_specifications.story: Security/compliance reviewer can prove restricted-profile compatibility end-to-end
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] Namespace `keystone-pss-restricted-test` exists with both PSS labels; Secrets `keystone-admin` (data.password) and `keystone-db` (data.password) exist in that namespace

test_specifications.story: Security/compliance reviewer can prove restricted-profile compatibility end-to-end
Operator developer gets a regression net for missing restrictedSecurityContext()
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] CR `keystone-pss-restricted` in test namespace has `spec.database.host=openstack-db.openstack.svc.cluster.local`, `spec.database.port=3306`, `spec.cache.servers=[openstack-memcached.openstack.svc:11211]`, no `clusterRef` on either

BLOCKER: Test triggers a manual Fernet rotation via `kubectl create job --from=cronjob/...-fernet-rotate` and asserts the Job reaches `condition=complete` (REQ-005)

BLOCKER: No changes under deploy/kind/, helm/keystone-operator/, or operators/keystone/ Go sources

CRITICAL: Resource names prefixed `keystone-pss-restricted-` everywhere (CR name, brownfield Database/User/Grant in openstack ns, manual rotation Job, OpenBao backup paths) to avoid collisions under parallel:4

implementation_notes:
Architecture & data flow:

```
[chainsaw runner host]
        |
        | apply 00-namespace.yaml
        v
[ns: keystone-pss-restricted-test]   <-- PSS enforce=restricted, enforce-version=latest
  + Secret keystone-admin
  + Secret keystone-db
        |
        | apply 00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml
        v
[ns: openstack]
  + MariaDB Database keystone-pss-restricted-db -> Ready
  + MariaDB User     keystone-pss-restricted-user (-> Secret keystone-pss-restricted-db-pw) -> Ready
  + MariaDB Grant    keystone-pss-restricted-grant -> Ready
        |
        | apply 01-keystone-cr.yaml (brownfield: host=openstack-db.openstack.svc.cluster.local, servers=[openstack-memcached.openstack.svc:11211])
        v
[ns: keystone-pss-restricted-test]
  Keystone reconciler creates (every Pod must pass restricted PSS admission):
    Deployment keystone-pss-restricted (API)
    Job        keystone-pss-restricted-bootstrap
    Job        keystone-pss-restricted-db-sync
    Job        keystone-pss-restricted-policy-validation
    CronJob    keystone-pss-restricted-fernet-rotate
    CronJob    keystone-pss-restricted-credential-rotate
    CronJob    keystone-pss-restricted-trust-flush
    Service, ConfigMap(s), Secret(s), Role/RoleBinding/SA, NetworkPolicy, PushSecret(s)
  Keystone CR -> Ready=True/AllReady
        |
        | kubectl create job --from=cronjob/...-fernet-rotate manual-rotate
        v
  Job  keystone-pss-restricted-manual-rotate -> succeeded > 0  (rotation Pod admitted)
        |
        | kubectl get events --field-selector reason=FailedCreate ... | grep -F 'violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"'
        v
  zero matches => PASS
```

Key design decisions and pitfalls:

1. CROSS-NAMESPACE clusterRef DOES NOT WORK. The issue text says to point `database.clusterRef`/`cache.clusterRef` at the openstack namespace's `openstack-db` / `openstack-memcached`. This is incorrect in the current codebase: `Database.ClusterRef` and `Cache.ClusterRef` are `corev1.LocalObjectReference` (name-only); `mariaDBResourceKey` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:70) looks up the MariaDB CR and creates Database/User/Grant CRs in `keystone.Namespace`, not in the MariaDB cluster's namespace. Therefore the test MUST use brownfield mode (database.host + cache.servers). This is the single most important deviation from the issue text and must be called out in the PR description.

2. SECRETS MUST EXIST IN THE TEST NAMESPACE. Bootstrap reads `keystone-admin` from `keystone.Namespace`; db-sync/User CR resolve `keystone-db` against `keystone.Namespace` (User CR is in openstack ns and resolves passwordSecretKeyRef in openstack ns). Pre-bake plain Secrets in 00-namespace.yaml with TEST-ONLY values, and replicate the password into a Secret in the openstack namespace for the User CR to reference. Do not introduce ExternalSecrets; that would require new OpenBao policy.

3. CHAINSAW IMPLICIT NAMESPACE. Chainsaw v0.2 generates a per-test namespace (`chainsaw-<random>`) without PSS labels unless `spec.namespace: ""` is set. Mirror tests/e2e/keystone/namespace-scoped-rbac/chainsaw-test.yaml which pins its namespace explicitly. Without this, the API Deployment lands in the unlabelled chainsaw namespace and the test proves nothing.

4. PSS LABEL SHAPE. Use both `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` and `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest`. Without the version label, kube-apiserver pins to the current cluster version, which can cause spurious failures when the cluster image is upgraded (a `latest` version label tracks the newest baseline, which is the regression net we want).

5. PUSHSECRET BACKUP PATHS. The fernet/credential PushSecrets push to `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{name}/{fernet,credential}-keys`; using a unique CR name (`keystone-pss-restricted`) keeps these per-CR paths distinct from sibling tests. The `push-keystone-keys` OpenBao policy (deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl) already covers the `+` wildcard, so no policy change is needed. PushSecret Ready=True is NOT in the `subConditionTypes` list (operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:105-118), so the test does not need to assert PushSecret Ready=True for AllReady to fire.

6. ROTATION POD COVERAGE. Only the fernet rotation Pod is exercised manually. The credential rotation and trust-flush CronJobs are NOT triggered; their Pod specs use the same `restrictedSecurityContext()` helper (verified by call-site grep), so a single rotation trigger is enough to prove the helper-vs-PSS contract. If a future reconciler adds a *new* unique Pod spec, this test catches it on first reconcile.

7. NAME COLLISIONS UNDER parallel:4. The brownfield Database/User/Grant CRs land in the shared `openstack` namespace. Use a unique prefix (`keystone-pss-restricted-`) and a unique database name (`keystone_pss_restricted`) to avoid colliding with `keystone-brownfield-*` / `keystone_brownfield`. Verify with grep against tests/e2e/keystone/*/00-keystone-cr.yaml and 00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml.

8. CHAINSAW CLEANUP. Default `cleanup.skipDelete: false` will tear down the test namespace and its resources, plus the brownfield Database/User/Grant CRs in openstack. Cleanup timeout is 3m (sufficient for finalizer-driven Keystone deletion).

References:
- security_context helper: operators/keystone/internal/controller/security_context.go:21
- helper call sites: reconcile_deployment.go:174, reconcile_bootstrap.go:157, reconcile_database.go:806, reconcile_fernet.go:315/328, reconcile_credential.go:318/331, reconcile_trustflush.go:124, reconcile_policyvalidation.go:174
- MariaDB resource key: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:63-72
- aggregateReady sub-conditions: operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:103-118
- chainsaw budget: tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml (assert 120s default, per-test 5m override allowed)
- pinned-namespace pattern: tests/e2e/keystone/namespace-scoped-rbac/chainsaw-test.yaml
- brownfield-mode pattern: tests/e2e/keystone/brownfield-database/{00-brownfield-db-setup.yaml, 00-keystone-cr.yaml, chainsaw-test.yaml}
- catch-block pattern: tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml (catch + describe + podLogs)
